title: Geographic hierarchy | Stats NZ
content:
## Geographic hierarchy
16 December 2022, 10:45am
The Statistical standard for geographic areas 2023 (SSGA23) defines the Stats NZ input and output geographic classifications.
The statistical geographies form a hierarchy of geographic areas which cover all of geographic New Zealand.
Figure 1 illustrates Stats NZ’s geographic hierarchy. Figure 1
Text alternative for figure 1, Statistical, electoral, administrative, and analytical geographies
Link to boundary viewer map in Stats NZ Geographic boundary viewer .
Link to Stats NZ Geographic Data Service .
Concepts, classifications, and concordances for the geographic classifications are available in Ariā for the following geographies:
- Statistical geographies Meshblock Statistical area 1 (SA1) Statistical area 2 (SA2) Statistical area 3 (SA3) Urban rural Urban rural indicator
- Statistical area 1 (SA1)
- Statistical area 2 (SA2)
- Statistical area 3 (SA3)
- Urban rural indicator
- Administrative boundaries Territorial authority Regional council
- Territorial authority
- Electoral boundaries Ward Māori ward Community board / local board Subdivision Constituency Māori constituency General electoral district Māori electoral district
- Community board / local board
- General electoral district
- Māori electoral district
See Statistical standard for geographic areas 2023 for further details.

## Text alternative for figure 1, Statistical, electoral, administrative, and analytical geographies
Figure 1, Statistical, electoral, administrative, and analytical geographies, shows the geographies maintained by Stats NZ that divide New Zealand into geographical areas. Meshblocks, which are the foundation for all statistical, electoral, and administrative geographies, support the electoral system, survey enumeration, and higher-level geographies. Moving up the hierarchy, statistical area 1s (SA1s) are the physical equivalent of a city or rural block and contain 100–200 residents; statistical area 2s (SA2s) with 1,000–4,000 residents represent suburbs or rural communities; statistical area 3s (SA3s) fit between SA2s and territorial authorities and represent suburbs in major, large, and medium urban areas. The urban rural geography defines New Zealand’s urban areas, rural settlements, other rural areas, and water areas. The administrative geographies – territorial authorities and regional councils – are defined by meshblock. Electoral geographies are defined by meshblock and are used for local and national government elections. Grids are analytical geographies that divide the country into areas of the same shape, size, and complement, but are not part of, the geographic hierarchy.
- Statistical standard for geographic areas 2023 (updated December 2023) 16 December 2022
